outlook.headline: Hyperscaler custom silicon demand broadens into 2026Q1
outlook.summary: The readthrough is still strengthening: most covered names cite hot or improving hyperscaler demand, and the mention trend has risen from 0.4x to 0.8x with positive momentum, suggesting custom-silicon programs are expanding rather than fading. Broadcom, Marvell, Astera Labs, Synopsys, ON Semi, Micron and others point to larger deployments, deeper design-ins, and more content per rack; the only nuance is that the latest mention level is slightly below the 0.9x peak, so watch whether activity holds after this run-up.

drivers.label: Custom XPU deployment scaling
drivers.detail: Multiple customers and large GW deployments point to stronger hyperscaler adoption of custom XPU platforms.

drivers.label: Broader design-in and engagement
drivers.detail: Earlier GPU/XPU customer engagement and stronger alignment with hyperscaler demand suggest more customized AI silicon programs are entering design-in phases.

drivers.label: Rising silicon content per rack
drivers.detail: Custom GPU/XPU platforms are increasing content density, supporting future demand for enabling components and infrastructure.

drivers.label: Improving real-world utilization
drivers.detail: Better XPU utilization in workloads indicates the economics of hyperscaler custom silicon are improving and supporting continued investment.

drivers.label: Wider adoption across the power tree
drivers.detail: Adoption across multiple XPU vendors points to a broader, healthier custom-silicon buildout.

risks: Mention intensity has eased from the recent 0.9x peak; a further drop would signal slower program momentum.
Neutral reads from Intel and Cohu suggest visibility is not uniform across the ecosystem.
If workload utilization or deployment cadence softens, the current hot-demand tone could normalize quickly.
